title: Rivian Q1 2026 Earnings Call Summary
text: Rivian Q1 2026 earnings call on April 30, 2026 reported revenue of $1.4 billion with 11% year-over-year increase. Gross profit was $119 million with 9% margin. Adjusted EBITDA loss was $472 million. Vehicle production reached 10,236 units with deliveries of 10,365 units. Cash position was $4.8 billion. The company initiated production of its mass-market R2 crossover, with employee deliveries underway. The R2 bill of materials is approximately half that of the R1 platform, with non-BOM costs expected to decrease over 50%. The Department of Energy increased loan funding to $4.5 billion, supporting a 50% capacity increase at the Georgia facility to 300,000 units annually. Management expects $2.55 billion in capital from Volkswagen Group and Uber in 2026, bringing total available liquidity to nearly $8 billion. Full-year deliveries guidance was 62,000-67,000 vehicles with Q2 deliveries of 9,000-11,000 vehicles. 2026 CapEx guidance was $1.95-$2.05 billion and adjusted EBITDA loss guidance was $2.1-$1.8 billion. Management confirmed the Rivian Autonomy Processor and Gen 3 hardware suite remain on schedule, with point-to-point autonomy capabilities targeted for year-end consumer rollout and Uber robotaxi pilots launching in two U.S. cities.
